props.label: This contract is drawn and binding between the Owner:
Work to be performed at the location of:

Term of Contract:
Annual Cost:
Contract Total:
Payment Terms:

This contract is executed in duplicate by the undersigned Owner and Contractor and is subject to the Scope of Work set forth in Section I, and Terms and Conditions set forth in Section II, attached hereto and made a part hereof. These <<$$TOTAL_PAGES>> pages are Agreed and accepted by:

A: Omega Fire & Life Safety (herein referred to as Omega in this contract) is an official tradename for Omega Corporation.
B: Any provisions or terms or scope of work found on any other documents such as customer purchase orders, acknowledgments, or any other customer form that is at variance with this Contract, shall not be binding against Omega. Any changes, modifications, or waivers to this Contract shall not be binding unless agreed to in writing in advance, and signed by an officer representing Omega. The parties hereto agree that Omega may alter or amend this agreement with 30 days notice to Owner.
C: All work as defined by this Contract shall be performed by Omega during normal business hours on normal work days (8 AM – 5 PM, Monday through Friday site local time excluding holidays) unless otherwise stated by special provision in this Contract.
D: This Contract assumes the covered systems in the List of Equipment of this Contract are in operational and maintainable order and condition, and the Owner agrees that Omega shall be notified promptly of any malfunctions or defects that come to the Owner’s attention as of the date of this Contract. Any repairs or corrections that Omega determines need to be made upon initial inspection shall be submitted to the Owner for approval prior to any repairs commencing. Should the client decline the proposed repairs and charges submitted, then all items included in the repair proposal shall be removed from the maintenance services covered by this Contract, and Omega shall be held harmless from all liability that may arise therefrom.
E: Omega’s obligation for repair, service, and emergency response if any, apply only to the components of the covered systems as described in this Contract.  Non-maintainable parts of the covered system including but not limited to cabinets, insulating materials, electrical wiring, field circuitry, structural supports, and other nonmoving parts are not included under this Contract.
F: During the course of the inspection process conducted under the provisions of this Contract Omega shall not be responsible for any failures that may occur as a result of the inspection when the failure is also due to the age, obsolescence, or normal wear and tear of the covered system.
G: Upon the issuance of an inspection report by Contractor to Owner in which defects or deficiencies have been noted, they are to be considered advisory in nature, and are intended to assist the Owner in reducing the risk of loss of property by indicating obvious defects or impairments noted in the covered system. They neither imply that no hazards or defects exist in the covered system, nor that all other elements of the covered system and its components are under control at the time of inspection. The final responsibility for the condition and operation of the covered system and components and equipment rests entirely with the Owner. The Terms & Conditions contained herein apply to any and all inspection deficiency repairs and quotes.
H: Unless otherwise specified, the repair/ correction of any deficiencies noted by the inspection report are not covered by this Contract. No corrective work will be undertaken without authorization by Owner. Repairs, replacements, maintenance, labor, and parts are not covered by this Contract, unless otherwise specified.
I: The reloading, upgrading and maintenance of computer hardware or software made necessary by any reason including but not limited to; neglect, Owner abuse or misuse, or through the neglect of others, is expressly excluded from this Contract as are such events promulgated through fire, explosion, Acts of God, lightning, storm, or by any other means beyond the control of Omega.
J: Expressly excluded from this Contract are systems and/ or equipment and components below grade, or exterior to the building, as well as components which are concealed and not accessible through normal work operations. All electrical wiring, conduit, system upgrades, or replacement of obsolete parts, systems, components and parts are outside of the scope of this Contract.
K: Devices such as smoke detectors, passive detectors, sprinkler system components, etc. purchased from Omega and installed by Omega which are added to the covered system, shall be covered by this Contract, unless modified by the provisions of the purchase order for such equipment.
L: Devices attached to the covered system which in the sole judgment of Omega are not compatible, or are installed in such a manner that it interferes with the proper operation of the covered system, regardless of its manufacture, shall be required to be removed and replaced upon notice to Owner by Omega. Failure to undertake removal process as requested by the notice or undertaking the addition of any third party device to the covered system shall be considered a material breach of the Contract. Omega shall not be responsible for any damage that may result to the covered system caused in whole or in part by such devices, components, equipment, or installations.
M: It is understood by Customer that any third-party programming and/or lifts, if needed, are not included in this contract, but shall be provided for an additional fee.

A: The parties intend that an independent Owner and Contractor relationship will be created by this Contract. Contractor is not to be considered an agent or employee of Owner or its agents for any purpose, and the employees of Contractor are not entitled to any of the benefits that Owner or its agents provides its employees. It is understood the Contractor is free to contract for similar services to be performed for other owners while Contractor is under contract with Owner.
B: The Owner agrees to purchase, and the Contractor to provide the services identified in this Contract subject to approval and continuance of credit approval by the Contractor. No waiver, change, or modification of the attached Terms and Conditions of this Contract shall be binding on the Contractor unless made in writing in advance and signed by a corporate officer representing the Contractor.

A: Contractor shall furnish duly qualified and experienced employees and supervisors or subcontractors to carry out the work to be performed by Contractor under this Contract. All personnel hired by Contractor shall be thoroughly screened, including police clearance, as permissible by law. Contractor shall comply with the instructions pertaining to conduct and building regulations issued by Owner, its agents, or other fully appointed officials designated by Owner. Contractor shall at all times enforce strict discipline and maintain good order among the workers and subcontractors engaged in work and shall cause such workers and subcontractors to observe all reasonable fire prevention, security and safety rules and regulations in force at the site of work.
B: Contractor shall provide adequate supervision at all times of the work which Contractor’s employees or subcontractors are performing under this Contract to ensure its completion and satisfactory performance in accordance with the terms of the Contract. In the performance of the work herein contemplated, Contractor is an independent contractor with the authority to control and direct the performance of the details of the work. However, the work contemplated herein must meet the approval of Owner and shall be subject to Owner’s general right of inspection and supervision to secure the satisfactory completion thereof.
C: Contractor agrees to warrant its workmanship and materials furnished under this contract for a period of thirty (30) days from the date of installation to be free of defects. Furthermore, the Contractor agrees to pass on the product or equipment warranty to the client, only to the extent provided by the manufacturer to the Contractor.

A: Access and Lack of Interference – Owner will provide timely access to Omega and its subcontractors at all times necessary for Omega and its subcontractors to perform the services as described in the Contract without delay. For the length of the Term of Contract stated herein, Owner will provide on-site parking for Omega and its subcontractors, in close proximity to the area in which work is being performed, without additional expense to Omega or its subcontractors. In the event that Omega or its subcontractors need to deliver equipment or materials, Owner will, upon request, provide access in any convenient place around the site, including the parking lot. In the event that work needs to be performed when the building is closed, Owner will, upon request, provide access within the shortest possible time consistent with safety. In the event of strikes, picketing, or other interference which is not caused by Omega or its subcontractors, Owner will provide safe access and entry to Omega and its subcontractors and will assure that Omega's and its subcontractors' personnel and vehicles are safe.
B: Maintain a safe working environment at all times to allow Omega and its subcontractors to perform the obligations set forth in this Contract, and take any corrective actions in a timely manner to restore a safe work environment for Omega and its subcontractors.
C: In the event of a covered system failure, the Owner agrees to undertake prudent, timely, and all reasonable measures in an emergency, to protect against personal injury, death, and property damage and to continue to take such measures until the covered system can be restored to normal operating parameters. Notification of any failure to the covered system in this Contract shall be made promptly to Omega.
D: In the event a covered system sends repeated transmissions to the Central Station, as determined by Omega in its sole discretion, one or more of the following actions may occur: a) Omega will dispatch a technician to Owner's property to correct the cause of the excessive transmission, in which event, Owner shall pay a service charge to eliminate the excessive signal transmission billed at Omega's prevailing rates; b) if technician is unable to 100% correct issue on site, for any reason whatsoever, Omega reserves the right to disconnect dialer and provide firewatch at Owner's expense until system corrections can be performed; c) Omega will charge an excessive signal fee per transmission, as determined by Omega in its sole discretion; d) Owner assumes all costs associated with any signal transmission deemed to be excessive in Omega's sole discretion.
E: If Emergency Services have been included in the provisions of this Contract, the Owner acknowledges that the agreement price expressly excludes travel expenses, parts and labor charges required as a result of accident, fire, storm, water damage, negligence, misuse, vandalism, power failure, current/ voltage fluctuations, lightning strikes, or failure in components, parts, or services provided by third parties. Owner shall be responsible for any of the additional costs listed in this Paragraph 4E.
F: Owner shall be responsible for notifying Omega and its subcontractors of the existence of hazardous material, risk of disease, the need for air monitoring or respiratory protection and/or equipment, presence of asbestos or asbestos containing materials, or any other potentially toxic substances Omega and its subcontractors could become exposed to in the process of performing its obligations on the Owner’s property or in the Owner’s facility as part of this Contract.
G: Omega shall have the implicit right to rely upon such client representations as they pertain to hazardous material and working conditions and as a result, Omega shall be indemnified and held harmless by the Owner for worker exposure to such conditions, as well as attorney’s fees and costs incurred as a result of said hazardous conditions whether or not the client has notified Omega and its subcontractors of the existence of such conditions. Any containment, removal or other measures necessary to remediate or remove these conditions shall be solely the responsibility of the Owner, and Omega and its subcontractors shall not be obligated to perform Contract obligations unless assured such conditions have been corrected.
H: Owner agrees to make timely payments to Omega and in accordance with the payment provisions of this Contract.
I: In the event an alarm is part of the system and monitoring covered by this agreement and is activated for any reason whatsoever, and Omega is assessed a fine, fee, cost, penalty or other expense by the monitoring company or by any court or local, state or federal government or governmental agency, Owner shall pay without recourse or reimburse Omega for all fines, fees, costs, expenses and penalties assessed against Omega.
J: Owner is responsible for all elevator coordination.
05 | Service and Maintenance Contract, & Add-on Services

A: Test and Inspection – Includes necessary testing equipment directly related to the system being tested, and labor for performing the inspection of the covered system. 
B: Service Call Coverage – Includes the labor only for service calls to the facility during normal working hours, Monday through Friday 8AM – 5PM, site local time, excluding holidays. These terms apply along with the other Terms and Conditions listed herein unless otherwise specified in Section I – Scope of Work. 
C: Panel Parts Replacement – Includes any and all components, boards, and/or CPU replacement within the confines of all control panel enclosures for the covered system. Labor, peripheral devices, and all other devices and/or components located outside the confines of the control panel enclosures are not included as part of this service. Batteries are not included with this service, unless otherwise specified, as they are consumable items. These terms apply along with the other Terms and Conditions listed herein unless otherwise specified in Section I – Scope of Work. 
D: Peripheral Device Replacement – Includes replacement of any and all peripheral devices located outside of equipment and control panel enclosures. Labor and replacement panel parts are not included as part of this service. These terms apply along with the other Terms and Conditions listed herein unless otherwise specified in Section I – Scope of Work. 
E: Covered System is defined as equipment, panels, parts, components, and/or parts as listed, specified, and defined in Section III - Equipment List and any addendum attached hereto.
F: The service level(s) the Owner requests has been selected after having reviewed the optional levels of protection offered and their related costs.

A: The Owner and Omega waive all rights against each other for damages caused by fire or other perils to the extent covered by insurance, except such rights as Omega may have to the proceeds of such insurance held by the Owner as trustee. The Owner understands and agrees that insurance covering personal injury and property damage on the Owner’s premises shall be obtained and maintained by the client, and that the recovery of any damages, or losses or injury claims arising therefrom shall by agreement be pursued by the client against his insurer for recovery. It is expressly understood, that the amounts paid to Omega under this Contract, are based on the value of the services to be provided, and the scope of liability as set forth in the terms of the Contract. It is expressly understood and agreed that Omega does not guarantee that no loss will occur, and Owner agrees that Omega is not responsible for any losses which may occur. The client hereby waives for itself and all others claiming for it under this Contract release and discharge of Omega against any and all hazards covered by the Owner’s insurance and expressly agrees that no insurance company will have any right of subrogation against Omega.

A: Omega shall not be liable for any consequential damages of any kind such as, but not limited to, damage from power surges, storm damage, business interruption or diminishment, Acts of God, loss of opportunity, or loss of business.
A: All services performed by Contractor under this Contract shall be performed in accordance with Section 1 - Scope of Work.

A: Omega may terminate this agreement for its convenience with 30 days notice to Owner. In the event that Omega terminates this agreement for its convenience, Owner shall not be responsible for any further fees or scheduled payments and/or cancellation charges or other fines or penalties assessed by the central station or monitoring company providing monitoring of Owner's alarm systems. Any payments made to Omega by Owner up to the date of termination shall be forfeited.
B: One-year contracts may be canceled with not less than ninety (90) days prior written notice by Owner, and the remainder balance of the contract and any cancellation charges or other fines or penalties assessed by the central station or monitoring company providing monitoring of Owner's alarm systems shall be due and paid without delay, and serve as the cancellation fee.
C: Multi-year contracts may be canceled with not less than ninety (90) days prior written notice by Owner; however, cancellation fees will apply. This also applies to multi-year monitoring agreements that have been signed separately from this Contract. Cancellation fees shall consist of the remainder balance of the current year of the multi-year Contract and any cancellation charges or other fines or penalties assessed by the central station or monitoring company providing monitoring of Owner's alarm systems will be due at the time of termination plus the original monetary value of any and all equipment provided for the Customer at no charge at the time of original installation under this Contract, as determined by Omega.
D: Owner shall pay Omega for each Service rendered pursuant to the terms and conditions hereof, unless and until the contract between Owner and Omega is terminated and no signals are received from the Owner's alarm systems. It is understood that it is the Owner's responsibility to continue facility monitoring through another source. Owner agrees and understands that Omega, at its sole discretion, can automatically charge a programming fee, billed at Omega's prevailing rates, within ten (10) business days prior to final termination date if signal transmission has not ceased. Owner further agrees to grant Omega access to facility and control panel to make necessary programming changes. Owner's obligation to pay this programming fee will survive the end of this contract term.
E: The Owner's obligation to pay any and all fees and bills will survive the end of the contract term until all fees and bills are paid.

A: Written notice shall be deemed to have been duly served if delivered in person to the individual or member of the firm or entity or to an officer of the corporation for whom it was intended, or if delivered at or sent by registered or certified mail to the addresses listed on the front of Contract by he who gives the notice.

A: The Owner and Omega each binds himself, his partners, successors, assigns and legal representatives to the other party hereto and to the partners, successors, assigns, and legal representatives of such other party in respect to all covenants, agreements and obligations contained in the Contract. Neither party to the Contract shall assign the Contract or sublet it as a whole without the written consent of the other.

A: Omega shall be entitled to an adjustment in the Price of any contract to which these conditions apply in the event, and to the extent, that the price(s) which Omega must pay for materials rise by 2% or more. In that event, Omega shall be entitled to the increase in material costs plus a proportional increase in its own overhead and profit.
B: Omega shall be entitled to an adjustment in the Price of any contract to which these conditions apply in the event, and to the extent, that the price(s) which Omega must pay for subcontractor services rise by 2% or more. In that event, Omega shall be entitled to the increase in subcontractor costs plus a proportional increase in its own overhead and profit.
C: Owner shall pay all costs of conversion of Owner's alarms to the Central Station utilized by the monitoring system and all charges made by any telephone company or other utility for installation, leasing and service charges for equipment necessary to connect the Owner's alarms to the telephone network or other signal transmission system network or transmit data or voice communication to the Central Station. Omega shall have the right, at any time, to increase the charges for services to reflect any taxes or charges imposed by any telephone company or other utility or other transmission system network provider or local, state, federal government or governmental agency relating to the services and which Omega or its monitoring service pays, and Owner shall pay the same.
14 | Cost of Enforcement, Failure of Payment

A: Failure of payment when due is a material breach of the Contract, time being of the essence. Without waiving this breach, in the event that Omega, through no fault of its own, is not paid in accordance with the Contract, then Omega may, upon three (3) days written notice to the Owner, stop the Work until payment of the amount owing has been received. If Omega demobilizes due to non-payment, once payment is made, Owner agrees to pay any remobilization costs of Omega.
B: In the event of a breach of this Contract by Owner, or if it becomes necessary for Omega to institute or engage in legal proceedings to enforce its rights pursuant to this Contract, Omega shall be entitled to recover attorney’s fees, collection fees and costs of the other party incident to any action brought to enforce this Contract from Owner. Owner, furthermore, waives the right to jury trial.

A: The Contract constitutes the entire agreement between the parties hereto and supersedes all previous communications, representations, or agreements either verbal or written, with respect to the subject matter hereof. No agreement or understanding changing the terms, scope or conditions of this Contract will be binding upon either party unless the same is set forth in writing and signed by a duly authorized officer of Omega.

A: If the Owner cancels or defaults on the scheduled payments, the remainder balance of a one (1) year contract or the remainder balance of the current year of a multi-year contract and any cancellation charges or other fines or penalties assessed by the central station or monitoring company providing monitoring of Owner's alarm systems shall be due and paid without delay, and will serve as the cancellation fee.

A: This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of Maryland, excluding its conflict of laws principles. In the event if litigation arising out of or relating to this agreement, the Parties consent to the exclusive jurisdiction and venue of the appropriate United States District Court for the District of Maryland or the Circuit Court of Maryland in the county where the services of Omega were to have been or actually were performed.
*Monitoring subject to 6% MD Sales Tax (tax not included in price at left)
140 E Main St | Westminister, MD | (410) 751-2220 | Fax: (410) 751-2221
